mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to expected corporate cash flows and pushes investors toward equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy remains near the lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 0.16 percent
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns following low FEDFUNDS readings are not above the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment from an elevated level signals improving labor income and lower recession risk, raising expected household spending and equity earnings, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while UNRATE is above 7.0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 7.7 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional hit rate or if the average triggered return is not statistically positive at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening final demand and revenue momentum for listed firms, so positive 3-month growth in retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value is 412403 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the conditional mean 20-day S&P 500 return after RSAFS growth crosses above 1.0 percent is not higher than returns after non-triggered months in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness signals lower capacity utilization and pressure on cyclical earnings, so negative 3-month industrial production growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases or if the return spread versus non-triggered cases is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead construction activity, durable goods demand, and bank credit growth; a sustained rise in housing starts therefore predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value is 894 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after HOUST 6-month growth crosses above 5 percent are not higher than the unconditional average in at least two-thirds of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices rise while the federal funds rate remains pinned near zero, real short rates fall and liquidity conditions ease, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth is above 1.0 percent and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 0.16 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS condition has no positive incremental return effect versus FEDFUNDS alone or if interaction-term coefficients are not positive in walk-forward models.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with falling unemployment confirm broad labor demand rather than participation noise, raising expected income growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is below 0 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 133852 thousand persons
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 7.7 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to improve 5-day hit rate by at least 5 percentage points over either single-series signal in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP below prior-cycle norms with low policy rates identifies an early-cycle recovery in which incremental demand growth has high equity beta, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 4-quarter growth crosses above 0 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value is 13638.1 billions of chained 2017 dollars
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 0.16 percent
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth under FEDFUNDS below 1.0 percent does not generate higher 60-day returns than positive GDPC1 growth under higher FEDFUNDS regimes.
